seo.title: Wisconsin CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Wisconsin - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity wisconsin, how to transfer CNA license to wisconsin, wisconsin CNA endorsement application

board.name: Wisconsin DHS
board.mailing_address: Wisconsin Nurse Aide Registry, P.O. Box 8916, Madison, WI 53708-8916

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: No expiration, maintained through work verification every 2 years

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Wisconsin.

steps.title: Complete a State-Approved Training Program
steps.description: Enroll in and successfully complete a state-approved Nurse Aide Training and Competency Evaluation Program (NATCEP) in Wisconsin. A list of approved programs is available on the Wisconsin Department of Health Services (DHS) website.

steps.title: Register and Schedule Competency Exam
steps.description: Register and schedule your Wisconsin Nurse Aide Competency Evaluation Exam through the official testing vendor, Pearson VUE, at www.pearsonvue.com/wi/nurseaides. Ensure your training program has submitted your completion information to Pearson VUE.

steps.title: Pass the Competency Evaluation Exam
steps.description: Successfully complete both the written (or oral) and skills portions of the state-approved competency evaluation exam administered by Pearson VUE.

steps.title: Automatic Registry Placement
steps.description: Upon successful completion of the exam and meeting all other state requirements, your name will be automatically added to the Wisconsin Nurse Aide Registry by the Wisconsin Department of Health Services (DHS). There is no separate application or fee directly to DHS for initial placement.

steps.title: Verify Registry Status
steps.description: After passing the exam, check your official certification status on the Wisconsin Nurse Aide Registry lookup tool at https://www.dhs.wisconsin.gov/cna/registry.htm to confirm your active listing.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Wisconsin CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $25. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: No, fingerprints are generally not required for CNA endorsement in Wisconsin.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Wisconsin?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Wisconsin while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in WI.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Wisconsin.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Wisconsin
hero.dek: Certified Nursing Assistants (CNAs) in Wisconsin are listed on the Wisconsin Department of Health Services Nurse Aide Registry after completing an approved training program and passing the state competency exam.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Wisconsin
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Wisconsin CNA certification.

official_links.portal_name: Wisconsin Department of Health Services Nurse Aide Registry
official_links.registration_steps: For initial certification in Wisconsin, there is no direct online application portal to the Wisconsin Department of Health Services (DHS). Eligibility for the Nurse Aide Registry is automatically granted upon successful completion of a state-approved training program and passing the Wisconsin Nurse Aide Competency Evaluation Exam, which is scheduled through Pearson VUE. For out-of-state CNAs seeking endorsement, the application is submitted via a mailed or emailed PDF form (Wisconsin Nurse Aide Reciprocity Application, F-82017) to the DHS Nurse Aide Registry.
